title: rubus foliolosus var. incanus
intro: Rubus foliolosus var. incanus, also known as the prickly thimbleberry, is a variety of the thimbleberry plant that is native to North America and is characterized by its white or pinkish flowers and edible, greenish-yellow fruit. The leaves and roots of the plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Rubus foliolosus var. incanus include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in herbal infusions and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
